title: Snakebite Envenoming Mortality And Antivenom Access Gaps In Rural Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring snakebite envenoming mortality risk and antivenom access gaps, linking WHO snakebite data, health-facility antivenom stocking data, and agricultural-population maps at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: WHO estimates 5.4 million snakebites annually, causing 138000 deaths and 400000 amputations. Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia carry 85 percent of the burden. Antivenom is the only effective treatment, yet stock-outs are common in high-burden districts and counterfeit products are widespread. Mapping snakebite incidence against antivenom availability at district scale has not been done systematically.

success_metrics.target: Every antivenom gap estimate links incidence, facility, and stocking data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
